author: amarco
text:
when I do a read\_by\_name and set the type to PLCTYPE\_STRING i get back the first character, if I purposely tell it to read PLCTYPE\_STRING \*9999 something extremely I get back the characters in string plus a boat load of \x0.  
I am wondering is there a method that can just return and arbitrary length string, or am I required to know the length?

Thanks

author: stlehmann
text:
You don't need to know the length of a string. Just pass PLCTYPE\_STRING and you should be fine. For further questions I kindly ask you to add a minimal reproducable code sample that demonstrates your problem.

Kind Regards  
Stefan

author: amarco
text:
I have a string in twincat defined as
VAR\_GLOBAL
MagicID: WSTRING := "BRIDGERIG";
END\_VAR
In my python code of I do
currentState=self.plc.read\_by\_name("GVL\_ExcitAxis.MagicID", pyads.PLCTYPE\_STRING)
currentState contains the letter ‘B’ only
Name
Value
Type
currentState
'B'
str
If I adjust python code to read 10 character
currentState=self.plc.read\_by\_name("GVL\_ExcitAxis.MagicID", pyads.PLCTYPE\_STRING \*10 )
I get an array of letters where each letter is separated the \x0 for instance
Name
Value
Type
▶
currentState
[b'B', b'\x00', b'R', b'\x00', b'I', b'\x00', b'D', b'\x00', b'G', b'\x00']
list
So it appears the in one case it returns a list where every other item in list is the character and the other case it returns a type string of one character.
So my question still remains on how to read strings from a plc variable without know the length of the string?
Marco

author: amarco
text:
Ok your comment made me think..
In Twincat there is a type STRING and a type WSTRING.
I was using twincat type WSTRING when I changed it to type STRING then python got back what I was expecting and it was of type string.
So looks like the library could use a PLCTYPE\_WSTRING.
Marco
